module_info.docstring:
Central registry for all hermes-agent tools.

Each tool file calls ``registry.register()`` at module level to declare its
schema, handler, toolset membership, and availability check.  ``model_tools.py``
queries the registry instead of maintaining its own parallel data structures.

Import chain (circular-import safe):
    tools/registry.py  (no imports from model_tools or tool files)
           ^
    tools/*.py  (import from tools.registry at module level)
           ^
    model_tools.py  (imports tools.registry + all tool modules)
           ^
    run_agent.py, cli.py, batch_runner.py, etc.

classes.name: ToolEntry
classes.docstring: Metadata for a single registered tool.

classes.name: ToolRegistry
classes.docstring: Singleton registry that collects tool schemas + handlers from tool files.

methods.name: register
methods.docstring: Register a tool.  Called at module-import time by each tool file.

methods.name: get_definitions
methods.docstring:
Return OpenAI-format tool schemas for the requested tool names.

Only tools whose ``check_fn()`` returns True (or have no check_fn)
are included.

methods.name: dispatch
methods.docstring:
Execute a tool handler by name.

* Async handlers are bridged automatically via ``_run_async()``.
* All exceptions are caught and returned as ``{"error": "..."}``
  for consistent error format.

methods.name: get_all_tool_names
methods.docstring: Return sorted list of all registered tool names.

methods.name: get_toolset_for_tool
methods.docstring: Return the toolset a tool belongs to, or None.

methods.name: get_tool_to_toolset_map
methods.docstring: Return ``{tool_name: toolset_name}`` for every registered tool.

methods.name: is_toolset_available
methods.docstring: Check if a toolset's requirements are met.

methods.name: get_available_toolsets
methods.docstring: Return toolset metadata for UI display.

methods.name: get_toolset_requirements
methods.docstring: Build a TOOLSET_REQUIREMENTS-compatible dict for backward compat.

methods.name: check_tool_availability
methods.docstring: Return (available_toolsets, unavailable_info) like the old function.
